name: Memory evaluation benchmarks (LOCOMO, LongMemEval, Deep Memory Retrieval)
context_primitives: Long-dialog and personalization benchmarks (e.g., LOCOMO) stress recall over many sessions. LongMemEval (community benchmark name cited by Zep and others) targets enterprise-style temporal QA. Deep Memory Retrieval benchmarks compare memory architectures on needle-like fact extraction across long histories.
deployment_pattern: Run vendor-provided harnesses or reproduce paper splits when comparing Mem0/Zep/Letta integrations; log precision/recall and latency separately.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: Mem0 paper discusses LOCOMO-class comparisons; Zep paper/blog discusses LongMemEval and Deep Memory Retrieval—cross-check methods sections before trusting leaderboard ordering.
ecosystem_integrations: Hugging Face datasets, academic releases, vendor marketing pages.
differentiators: Separates **temporal** reasoning from **static** RAG; many public leaderboards still overfit to literal string overlap.
significance_for_builders: Pick 1–2 benchmarks that mirror your product (multi-session prefs vs. enterprise policy updates vs. code repo memory) instead of generic NIAH.
key_timeline_or_milestones: Mem0 arXiv Apr 2025; Zep arXiv Jan 2025; benchmark names reused across vendor posts—verify dataset versions [uncertain].
